name: Kahlua Cheesecake Bars
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F. Grease a 13 x 9 inch pan.
2. Brownie Layer: Melt butter and chocolate in a small saucepan over low heat. Set aside to cool.
3. Mix flour, baking powder, and salt in a bowl.
4. In a separate large bowl, beat brown sugar, eggs, instant espresso coffee, and vanilla until well blended. Stir in the cooled chocolate mixture. Pour the batter into the prepared pan and spread evenly.
5. Cheesecake Layer: Beat cream cheese, sugar, and flour in a large electric mixer on medium speed until pale and fluffy. Add eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition. Mix in Kahlua or strong coffee, vanilla, and cinnamon. Pour over the brownie layer and spread evenly.
6. Bake for 20 minutes in the preheated oven until the top is almost set.
7. Meanwhile, make the sour cream layer by mixing sour cream and sugar.
8. After baking the bars for 20 minutes, remove the pan from the oven and carefully spread the sour cream mixture over the top of the bars. Return to the oven and bake an additional 10 minutes.
9. Allow the pan to cool completely on a wire rack before cutting into bars.
Makes 24 bars.
Per bar: 237 calories, 3g protein, 3g carbohydrates, 15g fat, 67mg cholesterol (with butter), 59mg cholesterol (with margarine), 83mg sodium, Exchanges: 1 starch, 1/2 other carbohydrate, 3 fat.
